reviews.review_description: Meditation ,  rest , lightness , transition , all are well design at this short film  , and at the end , when my dog left me , I saw a lot of boats without their owners , wihout dogs , and my boat stops by a rock and I trasfer as a fish , happy and soft swimming , and after that I know , all of these fishes are like me , there are transfored human ......WOW

It's a realtime rendered, well designed passive experience. You sit in a boat with a dog in front as your trusty companion transitioning between two worlds. You will see trees with falling leafes, massive statues, strange creatures and so on. If you like metaphorical landscapes or even just well designed VR worlds ypu won't be dissapointed.
Boring visuals set to annoying ambient music with a creative vision that was poorly executed. Pass!
When an experience of this level of quality & craftsmanship is out there for free, GRAB IT.  So that's what I did.  And you should, too!  🙂👍🏻
Wonderful artful usage of VR

One of the best experiences I've encountered in Vr.  Comfortable motion but an intense emotional experience when you think about this thing called life.  Although I don't know what is, Transition captures what can be in a beautiful way.  A wonderful experience for the mind, body and soul.

I enjoyed the experience and liked how my mind constantly tried to figure out where I was going and why? My conclusion was, afterlife. Only recommendation was a flickering on the last scene (once you 'flip'). My brightness was quite low, but the fast strobeing was apparently on the light background. Refresh rate? LCD tech, with that certain color of background causing the issue? Ive noticed this with other bright/white scenes. 
Thank you for making this experience for us!
Absolutely love it. It's peaceful and relaxing.
It crashes before launch, It has great reviews and Im dying to try it. S10 plus with Gear VR.
This is so beautiful and peaceful. I fire this up when I want a quote place to think. I’d love if the developer added a looping mode.

I can't describe my feeling after experiencing this wonderful app. It is very well made and graphics are really beautiful. Music is also very relaxing too.  Becoz of apps and experiences like this exists, i just love VR. I really don't know how they did it, but it's running super smooth on my oculus go and file size is also really light weight. Other developers should learn from this app and take advantage while creating games and experiences for oculus go. Kudos to develop of this app for keeping it Free!! So download now and be amazed.

This is a beautiful dream like experience. I couldn't help feeling like I've been here before.
I was sad when the dog left me, a companion like a dog I had years before.
The whole experience, music and visual, tugged at me, so very nice.

I loved this experience. Visually, it’s amazing, and it somehow provides a sense of peace and order through the journey we all must take one day. It feels private and introspective. Having the dog join us on the journey takes away the element of fear in this new place — like if the dog is calm, all is well and I can relax. The magical transition half-way through, the pacing, the beautiful music all made this a profound experience for me. One thought I had through this is that many of us — me included — are so disconnected from nature — is nature willing to accept us at the end. Instead of fish and trees and owls, would our afterworld be plastic, marketing billboards and mobile devices?

This is such an incredible work of art, fantastic job! I was in tears by the end, it’s such a beautiful experience and the music ties in perfectly. These developers and musicians should be so proud of what they’ve created. I showed my girlfriend and she was also amazed. 5/5.
Morbid and creepy
Reminds me of Relaxation River.
Stunningly Beautiful! Music is so lovely! Would highly recommend. Loved the little dog too .

I did not read the description, but from the beginning to end it felt like I was on a journey from this life to the next. I immediately put the peaces together and realized it was a journey of death. The creators of this short video have truly created a masterpiece visualization of how that journey might look and feel. I came
out thinking... did I just die and travel to the next life? Then I read the description about this experience being a metaphor of death.

I hesitate to use words like meditative and spiritual, because I know those a trigger words for some people. Let me describe it in concrete terms. You are in a boat and people are seeing your launch. You don't know where you are going, but a dog jumps in the boat with you as a faithful protector. You are at ease and the scene is beautiful and the music helps your flow. At the end the dog departs, and you float off into the atmosphere. I loved it!
It is very short and a little odd but i would imagine it would be brilliant on magic mushrooms lol
